title: Vegetation encroachment monitoring along corridors
summary: Satellite vegetation products detect canopy growth near tracks and overhead lines, targeting clearance work.
problem: Vegetation growing near tracks and overhead lines causes contact incidents, signal obstruction and leaf-fall adhesion problems. Manual line-side patrols are expensive, seasonal and inconsistent, missing fast-growing species between inspection cycles.
solution: CLMS tree-cover and vegetation-phenology products derived from Sentinel-2 can detect canopy change within a corridor buffer along the network. Change alerts above a defined threshold trigger targeted clearance work orders, replacing calendar-based inspection.

copernicusRole.detail: CLMS tree-cover and phenology products from Sentinel-2 are clipped to a corridor buffer and differenced over time to flag encroachment. Copernicus supplies the vegetation layer; change-detection processing turns it into alerts.
